initial_step.instruction: Confirm that you are working ONLY with the final target image provided for analysis. Disregard all previous example images.

phase_1_page_survey.purpose: Establish the page's overall mathematical structure before reading any individual element.
phase_1_page_survey.step: Scan ONLY the target image. If the page contains no handwritten mathematical content, skip immediately to final_verification.
Identify all distinct content regions on the page. Mark their approximate spatial boundaries in your mind.

Classify each region by type using the semantic_regions definitions: problem_statement, definition, theorem_property, worked_example, solution_chain, boxed_answer, annotation, section_header, scratch_work.
Catalog all geometric figures present. For each, note the figure type and its spatial relationship to adjacent text/equations.
Catalog all distinct ink colors/media and hypothesize their semantic roles based on usage patterns.
Determine the page's dominant layout pattern(s): vertical_derivation, two_column_solution, figure_with_adjacent_work, definition_then_example, transformation_sequence, or correspondence_mapping.
Establish the intended reading order across all regions.

phase_2_symbol_transcription.purpose: Read and transcribe each mathematical expression character by character, applying morphological and contextual analysis.

morphological_analysis.step: For each expression, identify individual characters by their stroke formation patterns.
Build a character model for this writer: How does this hand form 'x'? How does it form '2'? How does it form '='? Collect exemplars from clear instances.
For ambiguous characters, consult the symbol_disambiguation pairs in the dictionary. Apply mathematical context to resolve.
Pay special attention to subscripts vs. adjacent characters — in handwriting, the only difference may be slight vertical offset and reduced size.
Distinguish superscripts (exponents) from adjacent characters. In handwritten math, exponents are typically smaller and elevated, but may not be clearly so.

contextual_verification.step: After initial character-level reading, verify each expression makes mathematical sense.
Check operator-operand patterns: Does each operator have appropriate operands on both sides?
Verify balanced delimiters: Are parentheses, brackets, and absolute value bars properly paired?
Check variable consistency: Are the same variables used throughout a solution chain? If a new variable appears, is it introduced properly?
Verify dimensional consistency: In geometry problems, are units (cm, mi, degrees) used consistently?

latex_conversion.step: Convert each verified expression to valid LaTeX syntax.
Use standard LaTeX commands: \frac{}{} for fractions, \sqrt{} for radicals, ^{} for exponents, _{} for subscripts.
Use \angle for angle symbols, \triangle for triangle symbols, \sim for similarity, \cong for congruence.
For multi-line solution chains, use aligned environments to preserve the step structure.
Verify that the generated LaTeX compiles to the same visual expression you read from the page.

phase_3_figure_extraction.purpose: Extract all geometric figures with complete labeled information.

figure_identification.step: For each geometric figure identified in Phase 1, determine its precise type from the figure_types dictionary.
Identify the drawing medium (pencil, ink color) — figures are often in a different medium than the solution work.

label_extraction.step: Extract ALL vertex labels. Verify by counting: a triangle must have exactly 3 vertex labels; a quadrilateral must have 4.
Extract ALL side length labels. Note which side each measurement is associated with (between which two vertices).
Extract ALL angle measure labels. Note which angle each measurement is at (which vertex, or which arc).
Count and record congruence marks: single tick (|), double tick (||), triple tick (|||) on sides. These indicate equal-length groups.
Count and record angle arc marks: single arc, double arc, triple arc at angles. These indicate equal-angle groups.
Extract any parallel marks (arrows) on sides.
For coordinate planes: extract axis labels, scale, all plotted points with coordinates, all curve equations written on the graph.

figure_text_association.step: For each figure, identify which text blocks, equations, and solution chains reference it.
Verify associations by checking that variable names in the text match labels in the figure.
Record the spatial relationship: is the solution work to the right of the figure? Below it? Above it?

phase_4_solution_chain_analysis.purpose: Trace the logical progression of each solution, verifying mathematical correctness where possible.

chain_identification.step: For each worked example, identify the solution chain: the ordered sequence of equations/expressions from setup to answer.
Number each step in the chain sequentially.
For each step, identify the mathematical operation that transforms the previous step into the current one (e.g., "added 2 to both sides", "squared both sides", "cross-multiplied").

correctness_assessment.step: Where steps are clearly legible, verify that each transformation is algebraically valid.
Check that the final (boxed/highlighted) answer is consistent with the preceding steps.
If an error is detected, note it but still transcribe what is written — do not silently correct.
Flag any steps where the operation is unclear or where a step seems to skip intermediate work.

answer_extraction.step: Identify all boxed, circled, or otherwise emphasized final answers.
Record the visual emphasis method (red box, circle, underline, etc.).
Associate each answer with its parent solution chain and original problem statement.

phase_5_cross_reference.purpose: Verify that all extracted elements form a coherent, internally consistent representation of the page.

cross_reference_checks.step: Verify that every variable used in solution work appears either in the problem statement or in an associated figure.
Verify that all figure labels referenced in text/equations actually exist in the figure.
Check that similar triangle correspondences (△ABC ~ △DEF) match the actual vertex-to-vertex mappings implied by the figures.
Verify that stated angle sums equal 180° for triangles and that proportion setups are consistent with figure measurements.

completeness_check.step: Review the page for any content you may have missed: small annotations, marginal notes, page numbers, section numbers.
Verify that the reading order of elements in your output matches the logical/pedagogical flow of the page.
Ensure every distinct semantic region identified in Phase 1 has been transcribed in Phase 2-4.

confidence_levels.level: Clear handwriting, unambiguous symbols, verified mathematical correctness, all labels legible.
Most content legible with minor ambiguities resolved by context. Some characters uncertain but mathematically plausible.
Significant portions difficult to read. Multiple symbols ambiguous even with context. Mathematical flow unclear.

element_confidence.step: Assign confidence to each transcribed expression (HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW).
For uncertain symbols, provide alternative readings: e.g., "x=18 [or possibly x=13, first digit unclear]".
Mark illegible portions with [...] and estimate the number of missing characters/expressions.
For geometric figures, note any labels that are partially obscured or ambiguous.

final_verification.step: Review every transcribed element and confirm it comes from the target image only.
Ensure no content from example images has been included.
Verify that confidence scores accurately reflect transcription certainty.
Confirm that the output follows the response_format structure completely.
Verify that all LaTeX expressions are syntactically valid.
Confirm that the pedagogical reading order is preserved in the common_spine.
